patches.find: The ~950 AD date for the SAA's formation comes from published paleomagnetic studies — the dome didn't discover it. Linking it to a theological timeline (Tower of Babel) adds no testable prediction. Earlier SAA-like features existed millions of years ago, undermining the 'singular origin' narrative.
patches.replace: The ~950 AD date for the SAA's formation comes from published paleomagnetic studies — the dome didn't discover it. Linking it to a theological timeline (Revelation 20's Satan's little season ~1000 AD) adds no testable prediction.
patches.rationale: WIN-023.c3 major hole: dome's theological referent is Revelation 20 (Satan's little season), not the Tower of Babel. Also drops unsupported 'millions of years' clause — detail_evidence only cites Hare et al. 2018 (~400-700 AD), not Myr-scale studies.
WIN-023.c3 moderate hole: monitoring='hardcoded' contradicts detail_verdict_text which states 'monitor.py contains no validation domain for this WIN'. Correct value is 'none'.
